remarks: 2025 papers pending official upload; CSAT is qualifying

exam_stages.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (GS Paper I + CSAT Paper II)
exam_stages.stage_2: Mains – Written (9 papers: Essay, GS I–IV, Optional I–II, Language papers)

remarks: Prelims is common with CSE; separate Mains for IFoS

exam_stages.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (shared with CSE Prelims)
exam_stages.stage_2: Mains – Written (6 papers: General English, General Knowledge, 2 Optional subjects × 2 papers each)

remarks: Pattern revised from 2017 onwards (Prelims added); 2015–2016 had only Written + Interview

exam_stages.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (Paper I: GS & Engg. Aptitude; Paper II: Engineering discipline)
exam_stages.stage_2: Mains – Written (Paper I & Paper II: Engineering discipline, conventional/descriptive)

remarks: Two cycles per year; Mathematics paper not required for OTA entry

exam_stages.stage_1: Written – MCQ (English, GK, Elementary Mathematics; OTA: English + GK only)
exam_stages.stage_2: SSB Interview (conducted by MoD, not UPSC)

remarks: Two cycles per year; females eligible from 2022 onwards (SC order)

exam_stages.stage_1: Written – MCQ (Mathematics + General Ability Test)
exam_stages.stage_2: SSB Interview (conducted by MoD, not UPSC)

remarks: Single-day written exam (both papers); no Mains written stage

exam_stages.stage_1: Written – MCQ (Paper I & Paper II: clinical medicine, surgery, gynaecology, etc.)
exam_stages.stage_2: Personality Test / Interview

exam_name: Indian Economic Service / Indian Statistical Service (IES/ISS)
remarks: IES: 6 papers (Gen. English, Gen. Studies, Economics I–IV); ISS: 6 papers (Gen. English, Gen. Studies, Statistics I–IV)

exam_stages.stage_1: Written – Both (General English & General Studies: MCQ; Economics/Statistics papers: Written/Descriptive)
exam_stages.stage_2: Personality Test / Interview

remarks: Renamed from 'Geologist Examination' to 'Combined Geo-Scientist Examination' from 2020; papers for 2015–2019 are under old name

exam_stages.stage_1: Prelims – MCQ (Paper I: General Studies; Paper II: discipline-specific)
exam_stages.stage_2: Mains – Written (3 discipline-specific descriptive papers)

remarks: Not conducted every year; recruitment driven by vacancy notifications; 2020 exam held in 2021

exam_stages.stage_1: Written – MCQ (General English, Indian Freedom Struggle, Current Events, Indian Polity, General Accounting Principles, Industrial Relations & Labour Laws, etc.)
exam_stages.stage_2: Interview / Skill Test

exam_name: Central Armed Police Forces (Assistant Commandant - CAPF AC)
remarks: Paper I is MCQ; Paper II is descriptive/written — both on same day

exam_stages.stage_1: Written – Both (Paper I: MCQ – General Ability & Intelligence; Paper II: Descriptive – Essay, Précis, Comprehension)
exam_stages.stage_2: Physical Standards / Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

remarks: Pattern revised in 2022: Tier III is now descriptive pen-and-paper; Tier IV (Skill Test/CPT) is qualifying. Prior to 2022, 4 Tiers existed. 2020 exam delayed to 2021 due to COVID-19.

exam_stages.stage_1: Tier I – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, Quantitative Aptitude, English Comprehension)
exam_stages.stage_2: Tier II – MCQ (Paper I: Mathematical Abilities + Reasoning; Paper II: English Language & Comprehension; Paper III: Statistics / General Studies – Finance & Economics as applicable)
exam_stages.stage_3: Tier III – Written/Descriptive (Essay, Letter, Precis in English/Hindi) + Skill Test / Document Verification

remarks: DEO is not a separate exam — it is a post category within CHSL. Skill test speed requirement: 8,000 key depressions/hour for DEO; 15,000 for DEO Grade A (CAG). Conducted alongside LDC/JSA posts.

exam_stages.stage_1: Tier I – MCQ (same as CHSL: General Intelligence, English, Quantitative Aptitude, General Awareness)
exam_stages.stage_2: Tier II – MCQ + DEO Skill Test (Data Entry Speed Test: 8,000 key depressions/hour – Qualifying)

remarks: Paper II is qualifying (marks not counted in merit). Pattern revised in 2021: Paper I is now fully MCQ-based computer exam. Havaldar posts included from 2022.

exam_stages.stage_1: Paper I – MCQ (Numerical & Mathematical Ability, Reasoning & Problem Solving, General Awareness, English Language & Comprehension)
exam_stages.stage_2: Paper II – Written/Descriptive (Short Essay / Letter in English or any language in 8th Schedule) — Qualifying only

remarks: Recruitment is need/vacancy based; not held every year. 2018-19 recruitment was a mega cycle. PET/PST conducted by CAPFs/SSB, not SSC.

exam_stages.stage_1: CBT (Computer Based Test) – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Knowledge & Awareness, Elementary Mathematics, English/Hindi)
exam_stages.stage_2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Physical Standard Test (PST) – Qualifying

remarks: Streams: Civil & Structural, Electrical, Mechanical. Paper II is conventional/descriptive. 2020 delayed to 2021. Both papers on same day in recent years.

exam_stages.stage_1: Paper I – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, Part A: General Engineering – Civil/Electrical/Mechanical as per post)
exam_stages.stage_2: Paper II – Written/Descriptive (Conventional: Civil & Structural / Electrical / Mechanical Engineering as per post)

remarks: Skill test is qualifying; final merit on written exam marks only. Grade C: Group 'B' Non-Gazetted; Grade D: Group 'C'. 2020 delayed to 2021.

exam_stages.stage_1: Written Exam – MCQ (General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, English Language & Comprehension)
exam_stages.stage_2: Skill Test – Stenography Speed Test (80 wpm for Grade D; 100 wpm for Grade C) – Qualifying

remarks: Mega recruitment cycles: 2015–16 (35,000+ posts), 2019–21 (35,208 posts), 2024–25 (11,558 posts notified). Not an annual exam — cycle-based. Graduate & Undergraduate post categories notified together. 2022 protest-related re-exam controversy.

exam_stages.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness)
exam_stages.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness – higher difficulty; post-specific)
exam_stages.stage_3: Skill Test / Typing Test / CBAT (post-specific, qualifying) + Document Verification

remarks: Disciplines: Civil, Electrical, Mechanical, Electronics & Communication, IT, Depot Material Superintendent, Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant, etc. CBT 2 is discipline-specific. Recruitment is vacancy-driven, not annual.

exam_stages.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, General Science)
exam_stages.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ (Physics & Chemistry, Basics of Computers & Applications, Basics of Environment & Pollution Control, Technical Abilities – discipline specific)

remarks: Mega cycle 2018–19: 1,03,769 posts — largest single RRB recruitment. 2022 cycle: 1,03,769 posts again. No written descriptive stage. PET is qualifying only. Minimum 10th pass eligibility.

exam_stages.stage_1: CBT – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Science, General Awareness & Current Affairs)
exam_stages.stage_2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) – Qualifying (running, weight lifting tasks gender/category specific)

remarks: ALP and Technician notified together in same cycle. CBT 2 Part B (trade syllabus) is qualifying — marks not counted in merit. CBAT required only for ALP post. ITI/Diploma required for eligibility.

exam_stages.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Science, General Awareness & Current Affairs)
exam_stages.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ: Part A (General topics) + Part B (Trade/Discipline specific — qualifying)
exam_stages.stage_3: CBAT (Computer Based Aptitude Test, qualifying) + Document Verification + Medical

remarks: Technician Grade 1 requires CBT 2 (discipline-specific); Grade 3 does not. Notified alongside ALP in same recruitment cycle. ITI qualification required.

exam_stages.stage_1: CBT 1 – MCQ (Mathematics, General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Science, General Awareness & Current Affairs)
exam_stages.stage_2: CBT 2 – MCQ (General topics + Trade/Discipline specific) — for Grade 1 only; Grade 3 shortlisted from CBT 1 only

remarks: Conducted by Railway Recruitment Boards under Railway Protection Force. Mega cycle 2018: 9,739 posts. 2024 cycle: 4,660 posts. PET includes 1600m/800m run, long jump, high jump (gender specific).

exam_stages.stage_1: CBT – MCQ (General Awareness, Arithmetic, General Intelligence & Reasoning)
exam_stages.stage_2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Physical Measurement Test (PMT) – Qualifying

exam_name: Railway Zone-specific Technician/Technical Assistant (Zone variant) - Variant 3
Railway Zone-specific Technician/Technical Assistant (Zone variant) - Variant 4
Railway Zone-specific Technician/Technical Assistant (Zone variant) - Variant 5
IPPB / Regional Rural Bank (RBI-linked) Recruitment - Variant 3
Railway Zone-specific Technician/Technical Assistant (Zone variant) - Variant 1
Railway Zone-specific Technician/Technical Assistant (Zone variant) - Variant 2

remarks: Two cycles per year (Feb & Aug approximately). EKT only for Technical branch candidates. Flying branch candidates do CPSS (Computerised Pilot Selection System) at AFSB. Commissioned officer entry. NDA/CDSE entries are separate from AFCAT.

exam_stages.stage_1: AFCAT Written – MCQ (General Awareness, Verbal Ability in English, Numerical Ability, Reasoning & Military Aptitude) + EKT – MCQ for Technical branch (Engineering Knowledge Test: Mechanical / Electronics & Communication / Electrical & Instrumentation)
exam_stages.stage_2: AFSB Interview (Air Force Selection Board: Stage 1 – OIR & PPDT; Stage 2 – Psychological tests, GTO, Interview)

remarks: Graduate-level entry for civilians already employed. Candidates must be employed / self-employed. Not a full-time military service — part-time commissioned officer role. Exam conducted by HQ Integrated Defence Staff. Two papers, both MCQ.

exam_stages.stage_1: Written – MCQ (Paper I: Reasoning & Elementary Mathematics; Paper II: General Knowledge & English)
exam_stages.stage_2: SSB Interview (Services Selection Board: Stage 1 – OIR & PPDT; Stage 2 – Psych, GTO, Interview)
